title: lathraea tatrica
intro: Lathraea tatrica, also known as the Tatrican or Tatrican dodder, is a rare and endangered species of parasitic flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Lathraea tatrica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Traditionally, Lathraea tatrica has been used in herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
